question: For the quarterly data platform, what can be concluded about the trend of 'Complaints Received' over the year from this chart?

options.A: The chart only shows the share of complaints each quarter, so it does not reveal whether the absolute number of complaints increases over the year.
options.B: Complaints received clearly increase in absolute number every quarter because their segment grows as a percentage.
options.C: Complaints received clearly decrease in absolute number every quarter because their segment shrinks as a percentage.
options.D: Complaints received stay constant in absolute number throughout the year because each bar has the same total height.

explanation: The chart normalizes unrelated categories to 100% within each quarter. It can suggest that the share of a metric is higher in later quarters, but it cannot establish an absolute increasing trend without the underlying totals. A higher percentage share does not necessarily mean a higher absolute quantity.
